title: Panthers second-year LB Scourton carted off with right knee injury during opening camp practice
dek: Panthers second-year linebacker Nic Scourton was carted off the practice field with a right knee injury during the team's first day of training camp on Thursday. He went down on a wet field late in practice. The team has scheduled additional testing to determine the severity and timeline for his return.
author: The GoCheckMySports Desk
key_fact: Scourton, a second-year outside linebacker who recorded 47 tackles, five sacks, and nine quarterback hits in his rookie season, was carted off during the Panthers' opening practice and is undergoing testing to determine the extent of his right knee injury.
bottom_line: The extent of Scourton's injury will become clear once testing is complete. How the Panthers manage his potential absence on the defensive edge during a crucial season-opening window will be worth watching as camp continues.
body: Carolina Panthers second-year linebacker Nic Scourton was carted off the practice field with a right knee injury during the team's first day of training camp on Thursday, per ESPN's reporting. Scourton went down on a wet field late in practice and required evaluation and transport off the field.

The injury comes at an inopportune moment for a promising young defensive piece entering his second season. In his rookie year, Scourton played in 17 games with eight starts, finishing with 47 tackles, five sacks, and nine quarterback hits, per ESPN.

Coach Dave Canales declined to speculate on the nature or severity of Scourton's injury, saying he was "hoping for the best" but did not characterize the damage beyond that statement. The team has scheduled additional testing on Scourton's knee to determine what he is facing.

The emotional weight of the moment was visible on the sideline. Players gathered around Scourton on one knee after he went down. Coach Canales and every player on the roster individually walked over to embrace Scourton as he was leaving the field, according to ESPN's account.
Scourton remains scheduled for further evaluation, and the specific diagnosis, recovery timeline, and whether surgery will be required are all unresolved pending the results of that testing.
